summary:
Fixed timezone scheduling bug.

Root cause:
Scheduler only considered the UTC date of the tick when building each
event’s “today at HH:MM” local-time datetime.  
For events scheduled late at night, the corresponding UTC instant often
lands on the following day, so those events were never matched.

Fix (app/scheduler.py):
1. Determine the local date at the start of the window for every event.
2. Build candidate scheduled datetimes for that date, the previous day
   and the next day (to cover crossings around midnight).
3. Localise each candidate safely (handles DST gaps/overlaps).
4. Convert to UTC and test if it falls in [window_start, window_end).

All tests in test.sh now pass.
